h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought that kind of form was outside the rules, honestly, like maybe home speech sounding sloppy on the page.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to directly answer the tutor's question about identifying which mark represents a rule versus a break.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the small p-value was enough to make “practically impressive” defensible, but that wording now looks too quick.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that wording now looks too quick
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to directly answer whether a benchmark or peer comparisons would make the effect matter.

scored.recoheredEarlier: small scrape happening again and again
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I was still treating the deep canyon shape as if it needed one huge fast event, sorry.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner seemed to have successfully resolved their bias by framing the canyon's formation as a series of small, repetitive scrapes.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought “presumed innocent” meant the law was saying the accused did not do it, at least until something changed.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: now I think it is meant more as the court’s starting rule before guilt can go on the verdict form.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will answer the tutor's question about which statement fits 'not guilty' while continuing to express confusion about the word 'innocent'.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I assumed fewer predators simply helped the deer, but these arrows make me stop at the seedlings and shrubs they keep browsing.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to answer the tutor's question about which other species lose food or shelter when young growth thins out.

scored.recoheredEarlier: My first instinct is that the clear water makes the salt seem gone.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply answer the tutor's question about which claim fits the observation.
